articles.title: State Funding Rose in 2025 — But Public Colleges Still Lost Ground Per Student
articles.summary: A national finance report shows states put a record amount into higher education in fiscal 2025, yet enrollment rose even faster. That left public colleges with less support per student and renewed pressure on tuition, staffing and access.

articles.title: Measles Keeps Finding the Gaps: How the 2026 outbreak wave is testing U.S. vaccination policy and public health capacity
articles.summary: The United States is in the middle of its largest measles year in decades, with outbreaks spreading across states as officials warn that low vaccine coverage, cross-border transmission and strained public-health systems are keeping the virus alive.

articles.title: Hormuz After the Ceasefire: Why the World Still Cannot Treat the Strait as Reopened
articles.summary: The brief easing in the Strait of Hormuz has not restored normalcy. Renewed attacks, the U.S. blockade of Iranian ports, Europe’s cautious coalition-building and the U.N.’s institutional paralysis show how a single chokepoint can still reorder alliances, energy markets and deterrence across three continents.
